answer:
Let the speed of the car from A be \(x\) km/h and from B be \(y\) km/h.  
When traveling toward each other, their relative speed is \(x + y\), and they cover 70 km in 1 hour:  
\[
x + y = 70.
\]  
When traveling in the same direction, the relative speed is \(x - y\) (assuming \(x > y\)), covering 70 km in 7 hours:  
\[
x - y = 10.
\]  
Solving these equations gives \(x = 40\) km/h and \(y = 30\) km/h.

\boxed{40 \text{ km/h and } 30 \text{ km/h}}
